text_en: Reaffirming also the provisions relating to migrant women contained in the outcome document of the United Nations Conference on Sustainable Development 6 , and calling upon States to promote and effectively protect the human rights and fundamental freedoms of all migrants, in particular women and children, regardless of their migration status, and to encourage their active participation, as appropriate, in processes that contribute to decision-making, planning and implementation at all levels of sustainable development policies and programmes,

text_fr: Prenant note du rÃīle que joue l'EntitÃĐ des Nations Unies pour l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des sexes  et  l'autonomisation  des  femmes  (ONU-Femmes),  notamment  en  appuyant l'action  que  mÃĻnent  les  pays,  pour  ÃĐlargir  l'accÃĻs  des  femmes,  y  compris  les travailleuses migrantes, aux dÃĐbouchÃĐs ÃĐconomiques et mettre fin aux violences exercÃĐes Ã  leur encontre, compte tenu de son plan stratÃĐgique pour 2014-2017 7 , dont certains des six objectifs consistent notamment Ã  ÃĐlargir l'accÃĻs des femmes aux dÃĐbouchÃĐs ÃĐconomiques, Ã  lutter contre la violence Ã  l'ÃĐgard des femmes et des filles et Ã  amÃĐliorer l'accÃĻs aux services destinÃĐs Ã  celles qui en rÃĐchappent, et de ses politiques et programmes relatifs Ã  l'autonomisation des travailleuses migrantes,

text_en: Welcoming the agreed conclusions adopted by the Commission on the Status of Women at its fifty-seventh session, 8 and taking note, in particular, of the commitment, as appropriate, to continue to adopt and implement measures to ensure the social and legal integration and protection of migrant women, including women migrant workers, in countries of origin, transit and destination, to promote and protect the full enjoyment of their human rights and to protect them from violence and exploitation, to implement gender-sensitive policies and programmes for migrant women and to provide them with legal channels that recognize their skills and education, to provide them with equitable working conditions and, as appropriate, to facilitate their access to productive employment and decent work and their integration into the labour force,

text_fr: Rappelant la dÃĐclaration adoptÃĐe Ã  l'issue du Dialogue de haut niveau sur les migrations internationales et le dÃĐveloppement 9 , tenu les 3 et 4 octobre 2013, qui rÃĐaffirmait la nÃĐcessitÃĐ de promouvoir et de dÃĐfendre efficacement les libertÃĐs et les droits fondamentaux de tous les migrants, en particulier les femmes et les enfants, quel  que  soit  leur  statut  migratoire,  et  d'aborder  la  question  des  migrations internationales  par  le  biais  de  la  coopÃĐration  et  du  dialogue  Ã   l'ÃĐchelle internationale,  rÃĐgionale  ou  bilatÃĐrale  et  moyennant  une  stratÃĐgie  globale  et ÃĐquilibrÃĐe, consciente du rÃīle et des responsabilitÃĐs des pays d'origine, de transit et de destination dans la promotion et la dÃĐfense des droits de l'homme de tous les migrants et l'adoption de stratÃĐgies ne risquant pas d'aggraver leur vulnÃĐrabilitÃĐ,

text_en: Deeply concerned at the continuing reports of cases of migrant women and girls who are victims of serious abuse and violence, including gender-based violence, sexual, domestic and domestic violence, racist and xenophobic acts, discrimination, abusive labour practices, exploitative working conditions and contemporary forms of slavery, including all forms of forced labour and trafficking in persons,

items_en: 1. Takes note with appreciation of the report of the Secretary-General; 15

2. Invites Member States to consider ratifying or acceding to the relevant conventions of the International Labour Organization, including Convention No. 189 concerning Decent Work for Domestic Workers, and to sign and ratify the International Convention on the Protection of the Rights of All Migrant Workers and Members of Their Families, the Protocol to Prevent, Suppress and Punish Trafficking in Persons, Especially Women and Children, supplementing the United Nations Convention against Transnational Organized Crime, the Protocol against the Smuggling of Migrants by Land, Sea and Air, supplementing the United Nations Convention against Transnational Organized Crime, the Convention relating to the Status of Stateless Persons of 1954 18 and the Convention on the Reduction of Statelessness of 1961 19 and all human rights instruments contributing to the protection of the rights of migrant workers, and to implement the United Nations Global Plan of Action to Combat Trafficking in Persons;

3. Takes note of the reports of the Special Rapporteur of the Human Rights Council on the human rights of migrants submitted to the Human Rights Council at its seventeenth and twentieth sessions, 21 including the description of the vulnerability and difficulties of irregular migrants, including prejudice and limited access to protection, assistance and justice;

4. Encourages all United Nations special rapporteurs in the field of human rights, whose mandates relate to violence against women migrant workers, to improve the collection of information and analysis of areas within their mandates in which women migrant workers are currently encountering difficulties, and also encourages Governments to cooperate with special rapporteurs to this end;

7. 9. Exhorte en outre les gouvernements Ã  encourager vivement, sur le plan financier notamment, toutes les parties prenantes, en particulier le secteur privÃĐ, y compris les agences de placement qui participent au recrutement de travailleuses migrantes, Ã  mieux prÃĐvenir, notamment grÃĒce Ã  un soutien financier, la violence contre  ces  derniÃĻres,  en  facilitant  en  particulier  l'accÃĻs  des  femmes  Ã   des informations et Ã  des programmes de formation constructifs et tenant compte de la
items_en: 5. Calls upon all Governments to integrate a human rights and gender perspective into their legislation and policies and programmes on international migration, as well as work and employment, and to ensure that they are human-centred, in accordance with their human rights obligations and commitments under international instruments on international migration, with a view to preventing and protecting violence, discrimination, exploitation and ill-treatment of migrant women, and to take concrete measures to ensure that their migration and labour policies do not increase discrimination and, where appropriate, carry out impact assessments of such laws, policies and programmes in order to assess the impact of measures taken and the results achieved with regard to women migrant workers;

6. Calls upon Governments to adopt measures to protect the human rights of women migrant workers, including domestic workers, regardless of their immigration status, or to strengthen existing ones, in particular within the framework of policies regulating the recruitment and employment of women migrant workers, to consider developing dialogue among States on the development of innovative ways to encourage migration through legal channels, including to discourage illegal migration, to consider taking into account, in immigration law, a gender perspective in order to prevent discrimination and violence against women, in particular individual, circular or temporary migration, to enable, under national legislation, women migrant workers who are victims of violence to apply for residence permits without the intervention of the abusive husband or employer, and to eliminate inappropriate sponsorship systems;

7. Urges Governments to strengthen bilateral, regional, interregional and international cooperation in combating violence against women migrant workers, with full respect for international law, including human rights law, and to intensify efforts to reduce the vulnerability of women migrant workers, by providing them with effective access to justice and by promoting concrete action in the areas of law enforcement, prosecution, prevention, capacity-building, victim protection and assistance, by exchanging information and good practices on combating violence and discrimination against women migrant workers and by promoting alternatives to migration in countries of origin to sustainable development;

8. Also urges Governments to take into account the best interests of the child by taking measures or strengthening existing measures to promote and protect the human rights of migrant girls, including unaccompanied girls, regardless of their immigration status, in order to prevent such girls from being subjected, in the course of their work, to economic exploitation, discrimination, sexual harassment or violence, including sexual violence, including domestic work;

9. Further urges Governments to strongly encourage, in particular financially, all stakeholders, in particular the private sector, including employment agencies involved in the recruitment of women migrant workers, to improve their prevention, including through financial support, of violence against women migrant workers, in particular by facilitating women's access to constructive information and training programmes and taking into account the

text_en: gender issues, including issues such as the costs and benefits of migration, the rights and benefits to which migrant women are entitled in their countries of origin and employment, the general situation in the country where they are going to work and the procedures to be followed in order to legally migrate, and to ensure that the laws and policies applicable to recruiters, employers and intermediaries promote respect for and exercise of the human rights of migrant workers, in particular women;

12. Demande aux gouvernements de reconnaÃŪtre aux travailleuses migrantes, quel que soit leur statut au regard de l'immigration, le droit d'accÃĐder aux soins de santÃĐ  d'urgence,  de  veiller  Ã   cet  ÃĐgard  Ã   ce  qu'elles  ne  fassent  pas  l'objet  de discriminations fondÃĐes sur la grossesse ou la maternitÃĐ et de chercher Ã  remÃĐdier, dans le respect de leur lÃĐgislation, aux facteurs de vulnÃĐrabilitÃĐ au VIH auxquels sont exposÃĐes les populations migrantes et Ã  faciliter leur accÃĻs Ã  la prÃĐvention et au traitement du VIH et aux services de soins et de soutien y relatifs ;

13. Prie instamment les  Ãtats  qui  ne  l'ont  pas  encore  fait  d'adopter  et d'appliquer des lÃĐgislations et des politiques qui protÃĻgent toutes les travailleuses migrantes, y compris celles qui travaillent comme domestiques, d'y prÃĐvoir des mesures de contrÃīle et d'inspection, ou d'amÃĐliorer, selon que de besoin, les textes existants, conformÃĐment aux conventions applicables de l'Organisation internationale  du  Travail  et  autres  instruments,  afin  d'assurer  le  respect  des obligations internationales, et de mettre Ã  la disposition des employÃĐes de maison des mÃĐcanismes transparents et tenant compte de la problÃĐmatique hommes-femmes qui leur permettent de porter plainte contre leur employeur, de rÃĐsilier leur contrat en  cas  d'exploitation  professionnelle  ou  ÃĐconomique,  de  discrimination,  de harcÃĻlement sexuel, de violence ou de sÃĐvices sexuels sur leur lieu de travail, en soulignant que ces instruments ne doivent pas prÃĐvoir la punition des travailleuses migrantes, et demande aux Ãtats d'enquÃŠter rapidement sur toutes les violations des droits de ces derniÃĻres et d'en punir les auteurs ;

14. Demande aux  gouvernements,  intervenant  en  coopÃĐration  avec  les organisations  internationales,  les  organisations  non  gouvernementales,  le  secteur privÃĐ  et  les  autres  parties  concernÃĐes,  de  fournir,  en  vertu  de  leur  lÃĐgislation nationale, aux travailleuses migrantes qui sont victimes de violences l'ensemble des services d'aide d'urgence et de protection, y compris, dans la mesure du possible, des services qui tiennent compte de la problÃĐmatique hommes-femmes et soient appropriÃĐs  sur  les  plans  culturel  et  linguistique,  conformÃĐment  aux  instruments
items_en: 10. Encourages all States to remove obstacles that may prevent migrants from sending funds to their countries of origin or to any other country in a transparent and secure manner, without restriction and without delay, as appropriate, by reducing transaction costs and establishing gender-sensitive transfer, savings and investment systems, including for diaspora investments, in accordance with applicable legislation, and to consider, where appropriate, taking measures to address other problems that may prevent women migrant workers from accessing and managing their economic resources;

11. Calls upon States to address the underlying structural causes of violence against women migrant workers through education, information dissemination and awareness-raising, by promoting their empowerment and, where appropriate, integrating them into the formal economy, including by giving them a place in economic decision-making, and by facilitating their participation in public life;

12. Calls upon Governments to recognize the right of women migrant workers, regardless of their immigration status, to access emergency health care, to ensure in this regard that they are not discriminated against on the basis of pregnancy or maternity, and to seek to address, in accordance with their legislation, the vulnerability to HIV faced by migrant populations and to facilitate their access to HIV prevention, treatment and related care and support services;

13. Urges States that have not yet done so to adopt and implement legislation and policies that protect all women migrant workers, including those who work as domestic workers, to provide for monitoring and inspection measures, or to improve, as appropriate, existing legislation, in accordance with the relevant conventions of the International Labour Organization and other instruments, in order to ensure compliance with international obligations, and to make available to domestic workers transparent and gender-sensitive mechanisms that enable them to lodge complaints against their employers, terminate their contracts in the event of professional or economic exploitation, discrimination, sexual harassment, violence or sexual abuse in the workplace, and emphasizes that such instruments should not provide for the punishment of women migrant workers, and calls upon States to promptly investigate and punish all violations of the rights of women migrant workers;

14. Calls upon Governments, in cooperation with international organizations, non-governmental organizations, the private sector and other relevant stakeholders, to provide, under their national legislation, all emergency and protection services to women migrant workers who are victims of violence, including, to the extent possible, services that are gender-sensitive and culturally and linguistically appropriate, in accordance with the relevant instruments.

Engage les  gouvernements  Ã   ÃĐlaborer  et  Ã   mettre  en  Åuvre  des programmes de formation pour les fonctionnaires de police, les agents des services d'immigration et de police des frontiÃĻres, les agents diplomatiques et consulaires, le personnel  judiciaire,  les  procureurs,  le  personnel  mÃĐdical  du  secteur  public  et d'autres  prestataires  de  services  en  vue  de  les  sensibiliser  Ã   la  question  de  la violence  exercÃĐe  contre  les  travailleuses  migrantes  et  de  leur  faire  acquÃĐrir  les compÃĐtences et l'attitude qui leur permettront d'intervenir de maniÃĻre appropriÃĐe et professionnelle et en tenant compte de la problÃĐmatique hommes-femmes ;

items_en: 15. Also calls upon Governments to ensure that legislation and judicial procedures are in place to ensure women's access to justice, to develop and implement specific legal frameworks and policies for women, or to strengthen existing ones, in order to effectively address the needs and rights of women migrant workers, and to take appropriate measures to review existing legislation and policies in order to meet their needs and protect their rights;

16. Further calls upon Governments, in particular those of the countries of origin and destination, to introduce criminal sanctions to punish perpetrators of violence against women migrant workers and those who mediate them, as well as gender-sensitive remedies and judicial mechanisms to which victims can effectively access and which enable them to voice and take into account their views and concerns, at appropriate stages of the proceedings, including measures to enable victims to be present during the judicial process, to the maximum extent possible, and to ensure that migrant victims of violence are protected and not further abused, including by the authorities;

17. Urges all States to adopt effective measures to put an end to the arbitrary arrest and detention of women migrant workers and to take measures to prevent them from being unlawfully subjected to any form of deprivation of liberty and to punish individuals or groups who commit them;

18. Calls upon Governments to develop and implement training programmes for police officers, immigration and border police officers, diplomatic and consular officers, judicial personnel, prosecutors, public-sector medical personnel and other service providers with a view to raising awareness of the issue of violence against women migrant workers and acquiring the skills and attitudes that will enable them to respond appropriately and professionally and with a gender perspective;

19. Also calls upon Governments to ensure coherence in policies and programmes on migration, labour law and combating trafficking in women migrant workers, taking into account human rights, gender and human-centred development considerations, to ensure that the human rights of women migrant workers are protected throughout the migration process and to intensify efforts to prevent, prosecute and protect and support the victims and their families;
20. Urges States, in accordance with the provisions of article 36 of the Vienna Convention on Consular Relations, 22 to ensure that when a migrant worker is arrested, imprisoned and detained

items_en: 21. Invites the organizations of the United Nations system and other relevant intergovernmental and non-governmental organizations to cooperate with Governments, within available resources, in order to achieve a better understanding of the situation of women in international migration, to improve the collection, dissemination and analysis of data and information disaggregated by sex and age, with a view to facilitating the development and evaluation of migration and labour policies that include a gender perspective and protect human rights, and to continue to assist countries in combating violence against women migrant workers in a coordinated manner, ensuring their effective implementation, enhancing their impact and multiplying their benefits for women migrant workers;

22. Encourages Governments to develop gender-disaggregated and gender-sensitive policies for women migrant workers, in close consultation with women migrant workers and relevant stakeholders at all stages of policy development, and also encourages Governments to ensure that gender-disaggregated data and gender-disaggregated analyses are adequately funded and that the resulting policies are accompanied by measurable targets and indicators, time frames, controls and accountability, in particular for employment agencies, employers and civil servants, and that it provides for impact assessments and ensures, through appropriate mechanisms, multisectoral coordination within and among countries of origin, transit and destination;

23. Also calls upon the Governments concerned, in particular those of the countries of origin, transit and destination, to use the expertise of the United Nations, including those of the Statistics Division of the Department of Economic and Social Affairs of the Secretariat and UN-Women, to develop and develop national methodologies for the collection, analysis and dissemination of sex-disaggregated data that will enable them to obtain comparable data and establish monitoring and information systems on violence against women migrant workers and, to the extent possible, violations of their rights at all stages of the migration process, and to:
(a) Further study of the cost of violence against women, including women migrant workers, for women themselves, their families and their communities;
(b) To analyse the choices available to women migrant workers and their contribution to development;
( c ) To contribute to the improvement of macroeconomic data on remittances, which enable the definition and implementation of appropriate policies;

24. Requests Governments and international organizations to take the necessary measures to ensure that the declaration adopted at the High-level Dialogue on Migration and Development 9 held in New York on 3 and 4 October 2013 is duly taken into account in order to ensure that the human rights and human development dimensions of the issue of women's migration are addressed in the context of the United Nations High-level Dialogue on Migration and Development, held in New York on 3 and 4 October 2013.

text_en: adequately reflected in national, regional and international development policies and practices, such as poverty reduction strategies and strategies to accelerate progress towards the achievement of the Millennium Development Goals;

items_fr: 25. Encourage les organismes  des  Nations  Unies  et  les  institutions apparentÃĐes Ã  poursuivre et intensifier leurs efforts, Ã  promouvoir les partenariats avec toutes les parties prenantes, notamment les organisations de la sociÃĐtÃĐ civile, ainsi qu'Ã  coordonner comme il convient leur action Ã  l'appui d'une rÃĐelle mise en Åuvre des instruments internationaux et rÃĐgionaux, de faÃ§on Ã  en renforcer l'impact par le biais de mesures concrÃĻtes permettant d'amÃĐliorer les droits des travailleuses migrantes ;

26. Prie le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de lui prÃĐsenter, Ã  sa soixante-dixiÃĻme session, un  rapport  complet,  analytique  et  thÃĐmatique  sur  le  problÃĻme  de  la  violence  Ã  l'ÃĐgard des travailleuses migrantes et sur la suite donnÃĐe Ã  la prÃĐsente rÃĐsolution, en tenant  compte  des  donnÃĐes  les  plus  rÃĐcentes  compilÃĐes  par  les  organismes  des Nations Unies, en particulier l'Organisation internationale du Travail, le Programme des Nations Unies pour le dÃĐveloppement, ONU-Femmes et l'Office des Nations Unies contre la drogue et le crime, ainsi que des rapports des rapporteurs spÃĐciaux qui ÃĐvoquent la situation des travailleuses migrantes et des informations provenant d'autres  sources  pertinentes,  telles  que  l'Organisation  internationale  pour  les migrations, y compris des organisations non gouvernementales.
items_en: 25. Encourages the United Nations system and related agencies to continue and intensify their efforts, to promote partnerships with all stakeholders, including civil society organizations, and to coordinate their efforts appropriately in support of the effective implementation of international and regional instruments, so as to enhance their impact through concrete measures to improve the rights of women migrant workers;

26. Requests the Secretary-General to submit to the General Assembly at its seventieth session a comprehensive, analytical and thematic report on the problem of violence against women migrant workers and on the implementation of the present resolution, taking into account the latest data compiled by the United Nations system, in particular the International Labour Organization, the United Nations Development Programme, UN-Women and the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime, as well as the reports of special rapporteurs on the situation of women migrant workers and information from other relevant sources, such as the International Organization for Migration, including non-governmental organizations.

text_en: 70th plenary meeting 18 December 2013
